DECLARATION OF BRENDAN F. MACAULAY IN SUPPORT OF REPLY TO OPPOS TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGENT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC WICHNER, CRAIG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC AND CRAIG WICHNER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MR. WICHNER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND DEFENDANTS' ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES ONE, TWO AND FOUR AND MOOT AS TO ISSUE THREE. MR. WICHNER IS NOT ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT SINCE HIS MOTION IS ONLY BASED ON THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE YET HE IS SUED ALSO FOR HIS INDIVIDUAL PARTICIPATION IN THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL ACTS. AS TO ISSUE ONE, MR. WICHNER SATISFIED HIS INITIAL BURDEN THAT ADHERENCE TO THE SEPARATENESS OF MR. WICHNER AND ATM WOULD NOT PRODUCE AN INEQUITABLE RESULT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE. AS TO ISSUES TWO AND FOUR, DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THEIR INITIAL BURDENS AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUES. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE FIVE, SIX, SEVEN AND TEN ARE SUSTAINED. ORDER ON THE MOTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT ALSO RULED ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF THEIR MOTION, ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT; MEMO P/A FILED BY DEFENDANT ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC WICHNER, CRAIG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, AND CRAIG WICHNER DEMURRER. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE NOT PROPERLY SUBJECT TO JUDICIAL NOTICE IN CONSIDERING A DEMURRER. HOWEVER, AS THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FALSE REPRESENTATIONS WITH SPECIFICITY, AND REPRESENTATIONS MUST BE STATEMENTS OF FACT, NOT OPINIONS OR FUTURE PREDICTIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFY WHO MADE THE MISREPRESENTATIONS, TO WHOM, IN WHAT CAPACITY, AND WHEN. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH REQUISITE INTENT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-17-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, AND, CRAIG WICHNER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FALSE REPRESENTATIONS WITH SPECIFICITY, AND REPRESENTATIONS MUST BE STATEMENT OF FACT, NOT OPINIONS OR FUTURE PREDICTIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH REQUISITE INTENT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, NO AUTHORITY THAT THIS CLAIM RECOGNIZED IN CALIFORNIA. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brendan F. Macaulay, Esq., Nossaman LLP, 415-398-3600, for Defendant and moving party
Frederick L. Glasser, Esq., Law Office, 714-315-7022, Specially Appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC AND CRAIG WICHNER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Mr. Wichner's motion for summary judgment is denied and defendants' alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues one, two and four and moot as to issue three. Mr. Wichner is not entitled to summary judgment since his motion is only based on the alter ego doctrine yet he is sued also for his individual participation in the alleged wrongful acts. As to issue one, Mr. Wichner satisfied his initial burden that adherence to the separateness of Mr. Wichner and ATM would not produce an inequitable result and Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue. As to issues two and four, Defendants satisfied their initial burdens and Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issues. Defendants' Objections to Evidence five, six, seven and ten are sustained. Order on the motion signed in open court.
The court also ruled on Plaintiff's objections to defendant's evidence in support of their motion, order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

DEMURRER
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, and, CRAIG WICHNER DEMURRER. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Court does not consider extrinsic evidence not properly subject to judicial notice in considering a demurrer. However, as the 1st and 2nd causes of action, plaintiff must allege false representations with specificity, and representations must be statements of fact, not opinions or future predictions. Plaintiff must specify who made the misrepresentations, to whom, in what capacity, and when. Plaintiff must plead defendants acted with requisite intent. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, and, CRAIG WICHNER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action. Plaintiff must allege false representations with specificity, and representations must be statement of fact, not opinions or future predictions. Plaintiff must plead defendants acted with requisite intent. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to third cause of action, no authority that this claim recognized in California. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer overruled as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
